titel: Stronger protection for the independence of courts and judges
tldr: The government wants to strengthen the courts' independence from political influence through a new, consolidated law on courts and judges. The National Courts Administration becomes more independent and is renamed the Courts Board, and clearer safeguards are introduced for how judges are appointed and can be removed from office. Most of the rules are proposed to take effect on 1 April 2027.
lattlast:
Courts must be able to rule without being influenced by the government or by political interests. To protect that independence over the long term, the government proposes several changes, gathered into a new law on courts and judges.

The authority that handles the administration of the courts – today the National Courts Administration (Domstolsverket) – is given a more independent position in relation to the government and will in future be led by a board. At the same time it is renamed the Courts Board (Domstolsstyrelsen).

How judges are appointed is set out more clearly. The Judges Proposals Board (Domarnämnden) proposes who should be appointed, and the government becomes bound by the board's proposal. A new authority, the Board for Judicial Accountability (Domaransvarsnämnden), is to decide on serious matters – for example whether a judge should be removed from office.

In addition, a fixed retirement age for permanent judges is introduced, and the Chancellor of Justice will no longer supervise the courts. The supreme courts will in certain cases be able to sit in a special, larger composition.

The new rules are proposed to enter into force on 1 April 2027. However, the rules on who is appointed to the new boards are to apply already from 14 January 2027.
html:
In a state governed by the rule of law, it is fundamental that every citizen can have their case tried in a fair trial by independent and impartial courts. To create stronger and more long-lasting protection for the independence of the courts and judges, the government proposes, among other things, the following.

  • A new law on courts and judges is introduced.
  • The administrative authority responsible for the central administration of the courts is given a more independent position in relation to the government. The authority changes its form of management to that of a board-led agency. The change is marked by renaming the authority from the National Courts Administration (Domstolsverket) to the Courts Board (Domstolsstyrelsen).
  • Rules are introduced on a nomination procedure for the Judges Proposals Board and on the government being bound by the board's proposal in an appointment matter.
  • A new board authority, the Board for Judicial Accountability (Domaransvarsnämnden), is established to decide, among other things, on matters of removal from office concerning permanent judges other than justices of the supreme courts.
  • Rules are introduced on the retirement age for permanent judges.
  • The Chancellor of Justice will no longer exercise supervision over the court system.
  • Rules are introduced on the possibility for the supreme courts to sit in a special composition.

The new law and the other legislative amendments are proposed to enter into force on 1 April 2027. The provisions on the appointment of members to the board of the Courts Board, the Judges Proposals Board and the Board for Judicial Accountability are, however, proposed to enter into force on 14 January 2027.

Proposed decision by the Riksdag

The government's proposal:

  1. That the Riksdag adopts the government's proposal for a law on courts and judges.
  2. A law amending the Riksdag Act.
  3. A law amending the Code of Judicial Procedure.
  4. A law amending the Act (1971:289) on general administrative courts.
  5. A law amending the Act (1973:188) on tenancy and rent tribunals.
  6. A law amending the Act (1974:371) on labour dispute proceedings.
  7. A law amending the Act (1975:1339) on the supervision of the Chancellor of Justice.
  8. A law amending the Act (1994:260) on public employment.
  9. A law amending the Act (1994:261) on appointment-based employment.
  10. A law amending the Public Access to Information and Secrecy Act (2009:400).
  11. A law amending the Act (2009:966) on the Intelligence Court.
  12. A law amending the Act (2010:921) on land and environment courts.
  13. A law amending the Act (2010:1390) on the appointment of permanent judges.
  14. A law amending the Budget Act (2011:203).
  15. A law amending the Act (2014:414) on nominations to the Court of Justice of the European Union and the European Court of Human Rights.
  16. A law amending the Act (2016:188) on patent and market courts.
  17. A law amending the Act (2023:499) containing instructions for the Parliamentary Ombudsmen (JO).
